metadata.name: OneLayoutJson
metadata.description: Essay: why a single Layout JSON drives SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose, and Next.js — and the constraints this choice imposes. Four H2 sections. ~5-min read.

dataFlow.diagram:
flowchart TD
    VIEW[OneLayoutJsonView] --> VM[OneLayoutJsonViewModel]
    VM -- nextReadLinks --> VIEW

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_why_spec_first -> /concepts/why-spec-first, next_viewmodel -> /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the concepts_one_layout_json_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped concept URLs enumerated in transitions: /concepts, /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state, /concepts/why-spec-first.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /concepts/why-spec-first and /concepts/viewmodel-owned-state. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

transitions.condition: url is a spec-mapped concept URL or /concepts

notes: Third live essay. Flipping CONCEPTS_ENTRIES row 2 to 'live' is the last step.

One CodeBlock shows the same Layout JSON snippet alongside the three generated platform renderings (SwiftUI / Compose / React) in fold-out style — actually just three adjacent CodeBlocks with platform filenames.
